label: Detection Engineer
description: Builds and maintains detection logic for SIEM and EDR platforms — Sigma rules, KQL queries, SPL, and YARA signatures to identify attacker behavior across enterprise and cloud environments.

paths.name: Microsoft Sentinel Track
paths.rationale: Microsoft Sentinel is the dominant cloud-native SIEM. SC-200 and GCDA together provide the strongest detection engineering credential combination for Microsoft-heavy environments.
pros: SC-200 widely required in Microsoft-focused detection roles
GCDA covers detection engineering methodology beyond platform specifics
cons: GCDA relatively new — building market recognition

prerequisites.experience: SOC analyst experience strongly recommended. Detection engineers write better rules when they have operated on the receiving end of detections.

cert_sequence.notes: Microsoft Security Operations Analyst — covers Sentinel, Defender XDR, detection and investigation workflows. KQL is heavily tested — practice daily in Sentinel Log Analytics workspace. Microsoft Learn SC-200 path is comprehensive free preparation.

GIAC Certified Detection Analyst — detection engineering methodology, MITRE ATT&CK-based detection design, Sigma rule development, and behavioral detection across platforms. Most recognized dedicated detection engineering credential. Open-book — build notes indexed by ATT&CK tactic.

paths.name: Splunk Track
paths.rationale: For detection engineers whose primary platform is Splunk — still the most deployed enterprise SIEM in large organizations.
pros: Splunk certification widely required where Splunk is deployed
Clear progression from user to content developer
GCDA adds vendor-neutral detection methodology alongside Splunk-specific credentials
cons: Splunk cert path can feel granular with many smaller exams

prerequisites.experience: SOC analyst using Splunk is the ideal starting point.

cert_sequence.notes: Splunk Core Certified Power User — SPL query foundation. Required before security-focused Splunk exams.
Splunk Core Certified Consultant — advanced SPL, data modeling, and content development. The detection content developer Splunk credential.
Adds vendor-neutral detection engineering methodology making Splunk-built detections more rigorous and transferable. MITRE ATT&CK-based design and Sigma principles apply across all SIEM platforms.

paths.name: CrowdStrike / Falcon Track
paths.rationale: For detection engineers whose primary platform is CrowdStrike Falcon. CCFH validates advanced detection and hunting query development in Falcon — the core detection engineering skill in CrowdStrike environments. Paired with GCDA for the vendor-neutral detection methodology that makes Falcon-built detections more principled.
pros: CrowdStrike Falcon custom detection development is a high-demand skill
CCFH validates behavioral hunting query writing in the dominant EDR platform
GCDA adds vendor-neutral detection engineering methodology alongside platform-specific skills
Strong combination for detection engineers at CrowdStrike-deployed enterprises
cons: Requires Falcon access for preparation and exam
Less transferable to non-CrowdStrike EDR platforms

prerequisites.experience: CCFA recommended before CCFR. SOC analyst experience in a CrowdStrike environment is essential.

cert_sequence.notes: CrowdStrike Certified Falcon Administrator — Falcon platform foundation. Required prerequisite before CCFR and the starting point for all Falcon detection engineering work.

CrowdStrike Certified Falcon Responder — investigation and detection analysis in Falcon. Gives the responder perspective that informs better detection development — you write better detections when you understand how analysts investigate them.

CrowdStrike Certified Falcon Hunter — the core detection engineering credential in Falcon. Covers custom detection development, behavioral hunting query writing, and proactive detection strategy. The validation of detection engineering skill in CrowdStrike environments.

GIAC Certified Detection Analyst — adds vendor-neutral detection engineering methodology: MITRE ATT&CK-based detection design, Sigma rule development, and behavioral detection principles. Makes your Falcon detection work more transferable and more rigorous.

paths.name: Palo Alto XDR Track
paths.rationale: For detection engineers working in Palo Alto Cortex environments. Cortex XDR's custom detection capabilities and XSOAR's detection automation are growing rapidly in enterprise deployments.
pros: Directly applicable to Cortex XDR custom detection development
XSOAR skills add automation depth to detection engineering
GCDA provides the vendor-neutral methodology foundation
Palo Alto is growing rapidly in enterprise security operations
cons: Palo Alto detection engineering cert market is newer
Less recognized outside Palo Alto environments

prerequisites.experience: SOC analyst experience recommended. Palo Alto Beacon free training covers foundational knowledge.

cert_sequence.notes: GIAC Certified Detection Analyst first — establish the vendor-neutral detection engineering methodology before platform specialization. MITRE ATT&CK-based design and Sigma rule principles apply directly to Cortex XDR custom detection work.
Palo Alto Networks Certified XDR Analyst — Cortex XDR operations and detection investigation. The operational context that makes custom detection development meaningful.

Palo Alto Networks Certified XSOAR Engineer — detection automation and playbook development. Detection engineers who can also automate response to their detections are significantly more valuable than those who only write the rules.
